title: How to reverse the order of a list
task: Flip a column so the last value comes first — without sorting alphabetically.

excel.note: Excel 365/2021. Works for any values — it reverses position, not sort order.

google_sheets.note: Identical; SORT(A2:A4,ROW(A2:A4),FALSE) also works in Sheets.

explanation: SORTBY sorts one range by another: here the list is sorted by its own row numbers in descending order (-1), which simply flips it — cherry, banana, apple. Unlike SORT, this preserves the original arrangement in reverse rather than alphabetizing. In older versions without SORTBY, =INDEX($A$2:$A$4,COUNTA($A$2:$A$4)-ROWS($A$2:A2)+1) filled down does the same.
